---
image: "face_new.jpg"
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 17em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/kostadinoff
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=nQOcsJkAAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: person-vcard
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1414-8738
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kostadinovmd/
    - icon: fingerprint
      text: Scopus
      href: https://www.scopus.com/authid/detail.uri?authorId=57542360900
    - icon: diagram-3-fill
      text: Web of Science
      href: https://www.webofscience.com/wos/author/record/AEE-9300-2022
listing:
  id: recent-posts
  contents:
    - epinews-posts
    - "blog/*.qmd"
    - "15-minute-learning/*.qmd"
    - "learning-tasks/*.qmd"
  sort: "date desc"
  type: grid
  max-items: 3
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [title, date, description]
---

I'm Dr. Kostadin Kostadinov, a medical doctor, Chief Assistant Professor and researcher in the Department of Social Medicine and Public Health at the Medical University of Plovdiv, Bulgaria.

I hold an MD and PhD, as well as an MPH from MU–Plovdiv. I also completed an MSc in Economics and Finance and a Master's in Impact Assessment and Policy Appraisal from Sofia University, and a Master's in Statistical Business Consulting from the D. A. Tsenov Academy of Economics in Svishtov. I am currently pursuing a Master's degree in Law at Burgas Free University.

Alongside my academic work, I am training as a resident physician in Infectious Disease Epidemiology at MU–Plovdiv and serve as an Associate Editor of the Journal of Medical Internet Research (JMIR).

I am the founder of the [Social Epidemiology Lab](lab.qmd), a private research company dedicated to advancing evidence-based approaches in social medicine, public health, and health policy.

My academic and professional interests focus on **data science**, **epidemiology**, **policy evaluation**, and **health services research**.

This website serves as a space to share my research projects, my R packages (like [kkstatfun](kkstatfun.qmd)), [teaching materials](slides.qmd), 15-minute learning materials, learning tasks, and reflections on public health and social epidemiology.
All learning and task materials are developed in collaboration with my colleagues, although I bear full responsibility for any errors or omissions.

All opinions expressed here are my own and do not necessarily represent the views of any affiliated institution or organization.

---

## What I do

::: {.home-areas .grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.home-area}
<i class="bi bi-journal-medical"></i>

### Research

Research in epidemiology, health economics, and policy evaluation — published in peer-reviewed journals with reproducible code and open data.

[Explore my CV →](CV.qmd){.home-area-link}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.home-area}
<i class="bi bi-easel"></i>

### Teaching

Lecture slides, handouts, 15-minute guides, and interactive notebooks for public health students.

[Browse materials →](slides.qmd){.home-area-link}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.home-area}
<i class="bi bi-code-square"></i>

### kkstatfun

An R toolkit for medical statistics — from 2×2 tables to causal inference, survival, and HTA.

[View the package →](kkstatfun.qmd){.home-area-link}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.home-area}
<i class="bi bi-building"></i>

### Social Epi Lab

Independent analytics for universities, clinicians, NGOs, and public institutions.

[Learn more →](lab.qmd){.home-area-link}
:::
:::

:::

---

## Recent posts

::: {#recent-posts}
:::

[Epi News →](epinews.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
[Blog →](blog.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
[15-minute learning →](learning.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
